mission.title: prometheus: Incorrect queries results after Prometheus restart with memory-snapshot-on-shutdown enabled
mission.description: Incorrect queries results after Prometheus restart with memory-snapshot-on-shutdown enabled. This issue affects 12+ users.

steps.title: Identify prometheus troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your prometheus deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus -n prometheus --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review prometheus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus
```
**What did you do?**
Two days ago we have upgraded to Prometheus from 2.30.1 to 2.30.3 and **enabled** the experimental feature "memory-snapshot-on-shutdown (was disabled before).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Incorrect queries results after Prometheus restart with…
steps.description:
## Context

For a given label value pair foo="bar", we store a list call "postings list" which has all the series references that this label value belongs to. For a label-value pair, the list is sorted in the block's index and also in the memory.

But, they might not appear in order in the WAL. And
```yaml
prometheus, version 2.30.3 (branch: HEAD, revision: f29caccc42557f6a8ec30ea9b3c8c089391bd5df)
  build user:       root@5cff4265f0e3
  build date:       20211005-16:10:52
  go version:       go1.17.1
  platform:         linux/amd64
```

steps.title: Confirm Incorrect queries results after Prometheus… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus -n prometheus --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## Context

For a given label value pair foo="bar", we store a list call "postings list" which has all the series references that this label value belongs to. For a label-value pair, the list is sorted in the block's index and also in the memory.

But, they might not appear in order in the WAL. And when we replay the WAL, we simply add the postings in this list unordered.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
